addTile.diagnosis_head:
ROOT CAUSE:
`addTile` on `ConvolutionTile` is `private[raptor]` (`ConvolutionTile.scala:52`), so a standalone user snippet outside package `edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor` cannot call it even if receiver/type are correct. The docs also included a fabricated constructor hint using `Array.fill(...)`; your fa

area.diagnosis_head:
ROOT CAUSE:
`area` is **not** a generic method on all `LiteGeometry`; in the shown source it is defined on `LiteList` (the class that has `xs`, `ys`, and `numPoints`), so calling it on an arbitrary geometry value can fail at compile time. It also has a runtime precondition `require(numPoints >= 3)`,

compress.diagnosis_head:

`compress` is **not publicly callable**; it is declared `protected[raptor]`, so code outside package `edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.raptor` (or non-subclasses) will fail at compile time on `tile.compress`. The doc mentions internal scope, but it does not give a runnable package-context requirement, s

The failure is not in `computePointHistogramSparse`; it compiled fine. The snippet failed because `UniformHistogram.getNumPartitions` is a **method** and was referenced without `()` (`h.getNumPartitions.toLong`), which Scala treats as missing an argument list. This is a snippet/test harn
